satire_attempt: Tech lead in vintage Patagonia explains to junior that the SEV-1 page is 'just a sensor-contact, anityā, impermanent.' Junior watches as customers churn and the company misses payroll. Lead's pulse remains normal. They have decoupled their nervous system from the polarity of the signal. They have also decoupled the company from its revenue. Subscribe for more 'how I learned to read dashboards without responding to them.'
verdict_reason: The satire only works by inverting the verse's explicit falsifiability ('endurance is not avoidance' — the verse rules out the satire's premise). The verse names that the work *proceeds through* the operator — examination, acknowledgment, delegation — which is the opposite of what the satire mocks. To make the satire land, you have to ignore what the verse actually claims. PASS.
